steps.title: Mass Spectrometer Q-Exactive (Mass Spectrometer, ThermoScientific,...

steps.title: Cleaning the source
steps.instruction: Cleaning the source: Set source temperature to 50 °C Let it "cool" 01:00:00 Unplug the Ion MAx API source with HESI probe attached Unscrewed the Sweep Cone and the capillary Immediately plug another cleaned capillary Clean the sweep cone with wet Aluminium oxide power Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Rinse thoroughly with Water milliQ Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) In a glass beaker, cover completely the Sweep Cone and the capillary with Methanol HiPerSolv CHROMANORM VWR International (Avantor) Ultrasound during 00:10:00 Ultrasonic Cleaner USC - TH (Ultrasound bath, VWR, 142-0084, 230V 50Hz 370VA) Let it dry Replace the cone and the Ion Max API source with HESI probe Set source temperature back to normal

steps.title: Prepared a solution for Performance Evaluation...

steps.title: Metabolite Identification
steps.instruction: Metabolite Identification : m/z and RT of a chemical library ( IROA MSMLS, OAMLS, BACSMLS, FAMLS Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) ) checked with Thermo Scientific Xcalibur 4.416.14 MS² acquired on QC samples File exported : list of m/z and RT

steps.title: Features detection
steps.instruction: Features detection: Peak Picking on all samples is done by using Workflow4Metabolomics Automatic attribution of the precedent list Workflow4Metabolomics 25.0 Galaxy doi: 10.1093/bioinformatics/btu813

steps.title: Fusion of modalites = sorting redundancies
steps.instruction: Fusion of modalites = sorting redundancies: Only the best modality is kept for 1 metabolite, based on RT > Dead Volume (0-1 min) and/or lower CV(metabolite) on QCs

steps.title: Lipid Identification
steps.instruction: Lipid Identification : Lipids are identified based on an in-silico MS& spectral library on LipidSearch 5.0 ThermoScientific MS² acquired on QC samples Levels of confidence in the identification are classified in LipidSearch as: (A) “ all specific ions are assigned for the head groups and substituents” (B) “all specific ions are assigned for the head groups and some specific ions are assigned for substituents” or “some specific ions are assigned for the head groups and all specific ions are assigned for substituents” (C) “some specific ions are missing for the head groups and substituents” Compounds assigned as below D-level were not considered File exported : list of m/z and RT

steps.instruction: Fusion of modalites = sorting redundancies: Only the best modality is kept for 1 lipid, based on RT > Dead Volume (0-1 min) and/or lower CV(lipid) on QCs

steps.title: Cleaning the source
steps.instruction: Cleaning the source: Set source temperature to 50 °C Let it "cool" 01:00:00 Unplug the Ion MAx API source with HESI probe attached Unscrewed the Sweep Cone and the capillary Immediately plug another cleaned capillary Clean the sweep cone with wet Aluminium oxide power Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Rinse thoroughly with Water milliQ Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) In a glass beaker, cover completely the Sweep Cone and the capillary with Methanol HiPerSolv CHROMANORM VWR International (Avantor) Ultrasound during 00:10:00 Ultrasonic Cleaner USC - TH (Ultrasound bath, VWR, 142-0084, 230V 50Hz 370VA) Let it dry Replace the cone and the Ion Max API source with HESI probe Set source temperature back to normal
